📷 Example Photos
🦷 Wearing Aligners
How many hours per day?▾
Wear aligners 20–22 hours per day. Remove only for eating, drinking (except water), and brushing.
When do I change to the next aligner?▾
Follow your dashboard schedule. Only change when the aligner fits loosely and your dentist approves.
New aligner is tight — is that normal?▾
Yes — mild tightness for 1–3 days means teeth are moving. Severe pain or cracking: contact your dentist.
Can I eat with aligners in?▾
No. Always remove before eating or drinking anything except cold water.
🪥 Hygiene
How do I clean my aligners?▾
Rinse with lukewarm water each removal. Brush gently with clear soap. Soak weekly in aligner crystals. Never use toothpaste.
Aligners are yellowing▾
Remove before coffee, tea, or coloured drinks. Soak overnight in cleaning solution.
⚠️ Problems
Lost or cracked aligner▾
Contact your dentist immediately. Wear the previous aligner to hold progress.
Aligner has gaps / doesn't fit▾
Use chewies 5–10 min/day. If gaps persist after 3 days, send a photo report.
I have a lisp▾
Very common in the first 1–2 weeks. Reading aloud daily helps. Most patients adapt within 2 weeks.

Terms of Service
Last updated: 2025

By using BraceBuddy you agree that: this app is a patient communication tool only, not a medical device. You are responsible for the accuracy of information entered. All data is stored locally on your device. No data is transmitted to any server by this application. The app is provided "as is" without warranty.
Privacy Policy
BraceBuddy stores your personal information (name, DOB, phone, email, address, emergency contact, treatment details) only on your device. This data is used solely to generate check-in reports you share with your dentist. It is never uploaded to any server. Photos are temporary and not saved to your gallery. You may delete all data via Settings → Reset App Data.
⚠️ Medical Disclaimer
BraceBuddy is not a medical device.

This app is a patient communication and documentation tool only. It is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any dental or medical condition. All treatment decisions must be made by your qualified dental professional. In the event of severe pain, swelling, or injury, contact your dentist or emergency services immediately.

BraceBuddy was created to help dental patients stay connected with their treating dentist during aligner therapy.
